feedback.text: The games industry has been taking advantage of the lack of consumer rights for a long time. Ubisoft being sued for The Crew is certain proof that this is not ok. Many games exploit dangerous practices such as not being able to refund content that they can change after you pay for it. The Stop destroying videogames initiative is a clear warning call that something must be done to protect consumers. These videogames companies also exploit children with malicious business practices. I believe that the market is strongly in the favor of the businesses and the ones making games, not the consumer. Thank you for listening to some of my thought on this matter
